key_themes: Record commercial & defense engine orders and massive backlog
Consistent earnings beats with strong 2026 guidance (mid-teens services growth)
Aftermarket services revenue moat and recurring revenue visibility
Benefits from GE split creating a high-margin pure-play aerospace business
Overwhelmingly bullish analyst coverage with Strong Buy ratings and $330–$350+ price targets
sources: 25+ recent news articles & analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, Trefis, Zacks, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat)
30+ X posts analyzed (keyword search for $GE + aerospace/earnings/backlog; semantic search on investor opinions)
summary: GE Aerospace (NYSE: GE) is seeing strongly positive financial sentiment in April 2026, driven by an 18%+ revenue increase, record orders (up 74% YoY in recent quarters), a ~$190B backlog, and confident 2026 guidance for $7.10–$7.40 EPS, $9.85–$10.25B operating profit, and mid-teens commercial services growth.[[1]](https://www.trefis.com/stock/ge/articles2/596056/ge-aerospace-stock-surged-80-heres-why/2026-04-08)[[2]](https://www.tikr.com/blog/ge-aerospace-rose-8-in-the-last-month-heres-how-much-the-stock-could-climb-in-2026)[[3]](https://x.com/fiscal_ai/status/2014397697281909075) The stock has surged roughly 70–80% over the past year, reaching all-time highs near $330–$350 earlier in 2026, as the post-split pure-play aerospace focus, defense contract wins, and sticky aftermarket moat (80K+ engine installed base) reward investors.[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/1-billion-reason-buy-ge-155100607.html)[[5]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/greatspeculations/2026/03/10/whats-behind-ge-stocks-massive-rally/) Ahead of Q1 2026 earnings (April 21), consensus calls for ~$1.63 EPS (+9% YoY) and ~$10.6B revenue (+18%), with analysts maintaining Strong Buy ratings and targets averaging ~$350 (highs to $425). Occasional pullbacks reflect profit-taking or moderated growth expectations, but overall momentum and visibility remain excellent.[[6]](https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2901334/ahead-of-ge-ge-q1-earnings-get-ready-with-wall-street-estimates-for-key-metrics)[[7]](https://stockanalysis.com/stocks/ge/)
